articles.title: ESA Awards Initial €700M Contract for Next-Gen Sentinel-1 Satellites
articles.summary:
The European Space Agency has awarded Thales Alenia Space in Italy a contract to build two identical Sentinel-1 Next Generation satellites. The contract represents the first phase of the project and is worth €700 million. A total of four Sentinel-1 first-generation satellites were launched between 2014 and 2025, with three remaining operational after Sentinel-1B was […]
The post ESA Awards Initial €700M Contract for Next-Gen Sentinel-1 Satellites appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: NASA Webb Finds Strongest Evidence Yet for ‘Black Hole Stars’
articles.summary: The complex puzzle known as little red dots has become more complete since their initial discovery by NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope in 2022. Now a particular little red dot’s spectrum is helping connect many of the pieces. A team of astronomers led by Vasily Kokorev at the University of Texas at Austin identified the […]

articles.title: Jim Irons, Former Landsat Project Scientist, Wins Pecora Award
articles.summary: Landsat’s Jim Irons won the prestigious William T. Pecora Award. Irons, now an emeritus scientist at NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, played an integral role in shaping the Landsat program into what it is today.

articles.title: Orbital raises $5 million to join orbital data center race
articles.summary:
A Los Angeles-based startup has raised $5 million to fund an in-orbit computing demonstration next year, ahead of plans to deploy more than 100,000 orbital data centers to meet surging demand for AI infrastructure.
The post Orbital raises $5 million to join orbital data center race appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: GLOBE Mission Earth Educators Participate in Land Cover Community of Practice
articles.summary: During the 2025-2026 school year, educators from the NASA Science Activation Program’s GLOBE (Global Learning and Observation to Benefit the Environment) Mission Earth project participated in a specialized Community of Practice led by NASA Langley Research Center to refine how students interact with NASA’s land cover data (MODIS, Landsat, and Sentinel-2).

articles.title: NASA’s CloudCube Pioneers Miniaturized Radar to Study Clouds, Precipitation
articles.summary: A compact, multifrequency radar built by a team at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory will make it easier to collect information about dynamic cloud systems. Called CloudCube, this new instrument simultaneously probes the atmosphere with three radar signals, spanning 36 to 240 GHz, for optimized sensitivity to a wide range of water droplet and ice particle sizes.
